concepts.temperature: Temperature is a measure of the average kinetic energy of particles in a system.
concepts.heat: Heat is the transfer of thermal energy between systems due to a temperature difference.
concepts.work: Work in thermodynamics is energy transfer via macroscopic forces.
concepts.internal_energy: Internal energy is the total microscopic kinetic and potential energy of a system.
concepts.entropy: Entropy is a measure of the number of accessible microstates; it quantifies disorder.
concepts.zeroth_law: The zeroth law: if A is in thermal equilibrium with B, and B with C, then A with C.
concepts.first_law: The first law: change in internal energy equals heat added minus work done.
concepts.second_law: The second law: total entropy of an isolated system never decreases.
concepts.third_law: The third law: as temperature approaches absolute zero, entropy approaches a minimum.
concepts.equilibrium: Thermal equilibrium is the state where no net heat flows between systems.
concepts.carnot_cycle: The Carnot cycle is an idealized cycle that sets maximum efficiency for heat engines.
concepts.efficiency: Thermodynamic efficiency is the ratio of useful work output to total heat input.
concepts.heat_engine: A heat engine converts heat from a hot reservoir into work.
concepts.refrigerator: A refrigerator transfers heat from cold to hot reservoir, requiring work input.
concepts.enthalpy: Enthalpy H = U + PV measures heat content at constant pressure.
concepts.free_energy: Gibbs free energy G = H - TS determines spontaneity at constant T and P.
concepts.reversible: A reversible process proceeds through equilibrium states.
concepts.irreversible: An irreversible process generates entropy and cannot be exactly reversed.
concepts.absolute_zero: Absolute zero (0 K) is the theoretical lowest temperature.
concepts.phase_transition: A phase transition is a change of state at specific temperature and pressure.

concepts.variation: Variation: differences in traits among individuals in a population.
concepts.heredity: Heredity: passing of traits from parents to offspring through genes.
concepts.mutation: Mutation: random change in DNA introducing new genetic variants.
concepts.natural_selection: Natural selection: differential survival and reproduction based on fitness.
concepts.fitness: Fitness: relative reproductive success in a given environment.
concepts.adaptation: Adaptation: trait shaped by selection that improves fitness.
concepts.gene: A gene: segment of DNA encoding instructions for a functional product.
concepts.allele: An allele: one of multiple forms of a gene at a specific locus.
concepts.genotype: Genotype: the complete set of alleles an organism carries.
concepts.phenotype: Phenotype: observable characteristics from genotype-environment interaction.
concepts.population: A population: group of interbreeding organisms of the same species.
concepts.genetic_drift: Genetic drift: random allele frequency changes, stronger in small populations.
concepts.speciation: Speciation: formation of new species through reproductive isolation.
concepts.reproductive_isolation: Reproductive isolation: prevents gene flow between populations.
concepts.sexual_selection: Sexual selection: favors traits increasing mating success.
concepts.gene_flow: Gene flow: transfer of alleles between populations through migration.
concepts.common_descent: Common descent: all species share ancestors, forming a tree of life.
concepts.fossil_record: Fossil record: physical evidence of organisms from past periods.
concepts.homology: Homology: shared structures inherited from a common ancestor.
concepts.coevolution: Coevolution: reciprocal evolutionary change between interacting species.

concepts.steam_engine: The steam engine converted heat into mechanical motion.
concepts.textile_mills: Textile mills centralized spinning and weaving.
concepts.coal_mining: Coal mining expanded to fuel steam engines and iron smelting.
concepts.iron_production: Iron production grew with coke-smelting techniques.
concepts.factory_system: The factory system concentrated workers and machines in buildings.
concepts.urbanization: Urbanization: mass migration from rural areas to industrial cities.
concepts.child_labor: Child labor was widespread in factories and mines.
concepts.labor_unions: Labor unions formed to bargain for better conditions.
concepts.enclosure: Enclosure acts privatized common lands, displacing farmers.
concepts.population_growth: Population growth accelerated from better nutrition.
concepts.canals: Canal networks enabled cheap bulk transport.
concepts.division_of_labor: Division of labor broke production into specialized tasks.
concepts.pollution: Industrial pollution fouled air and water.
concepts.class_structure: Class structure polarized into owners and workers.
concepts.global_trade: Global trade expanded with manufactured goods.

concepts.set: A set is a well-defined collection of distinct elements.
concepts.binary_operation: A binary operation combines two elements to produce a third.
concepts.closure: Closure: applying the operation to group elements yields a group element.
concepts.identity_element: The identity element e satisfies a*e = e*a = a.
concepts.inverse: For every a, there exists inverse a_inv such that a*a_inv = e.
concepts.group: A group: set + operation satisfying closure, associativity, identity, inverse.
concepts.abelian: An abelian group additionally satisfies a*b = b*a.
concepts.subgroup: A subgroup is a subset that is itself a group.
concepts.cyclic_group: A cyclic group is generated by a single element.
concepts.order: Order of a group: its number of elements.
concepts.coset: A coset: set formed by multiplying subgroup elements by a fixed element.
concepts.normal_subgroup: A normal subgroup N satisfies gNg_inv = N for all g.
concepts.quotient_group: A quotient group G/N is formed from cosets of normal subgroup N.
concepts.homomorphism: A homomorphism preserves the operation: f(a*b) = f(a)*f(b).
concepts.isomorphism: An isomorphism is a bijective homomorphism.
concepts.kernel: The kernel of f is {g: f(g) = identity}; always a normal subgroup.
concepts.lagrange_theorem: Lagrange: the order of a subgroup divides the order of the group.
concepts.permutation_group: A permutation group: bijections on a set, under composition.
concepts.symmetry: Symmetry transformations form a group; group theory = math of symmetry.
